paragraphs.text: Dear Sir or Madam, As a long-time enjoyer of many video games, I always felt powerless seeing a downwards trend in quality as spendings were cut while manipulative patterns such as gambling or social pressure grew for the sake of profit. Following the "Stop Killing Games" movement and hearing news of the Digital Fairness Act is, consequently, a great relief as it signals a chance to resolve the issues regarding dark patterns. With that said, I humbly ask for an additional point for the Digital Fairness Act regarding Video Games that are constantly maintained online, and that their contents don't have to become inaccessible with End of Support. Games with ongoing support and development such as "Warframe" continue to thrive thanks to their transparency and openness with their community of players and customers, however such games with ongoing lifecycles are at risk to major or complete loss of functionality when support eventually comes to an end, in this case rendering 12 years of ongoing open beta developments, along with its large share of Art, inaccessible. Many games like this are facing similar risks in the near or far future. Some games such as "The Crew" have already become inaccessible to the customers which paid a minimum of 60 for a product that is now non-functional. While it's not necessarily always avoidable, this particular case could have been handled notably fairer towards customers than to permanently disable all access to a game that could have been fully functional offline. For that reason, many who purchased the Game feel as though it was taken away from them, thus feeling treated unfairly. In an interest to prevent the continued exploitation of similar tactics, and the protection of Council Directive 93/13/EEC on unfair terms in consumer contracts, it would make me and many others feel safer about my invested time and resources to see the DFA expanded upon to protect against the permanent deactivation of readilly sold products, more specifically Video Games. For further reference I want to inform about the "Stop Killing Games" movement with the same goal in mind, the Channel "Accursed Farms" on YouTube informing about the movement and its updates in great detail. I thank you for taking the time to read my feedback in full, and am looking forward to seeing developments towards a fairer environment for customers.
